paragraphs: Health, Fitness, and Lifestyle Overlay Specification
DOC-17 | Version 1.0 | Meal Planning RE Technical Docs v1
1. Purpose and Scope
Defines overlays for diabetic, weight loss, high protein, gym/fitness, elderly digestion, pregnancy, postpartum, child growth, recovery, and modern healthy lifestyle patterns.

This document is part of the canonical 28-document technical system. It should be read in the order defined in DOC-00. It is written for an AI coding agent that must implement the agreed recommendation engine exactly, without inventing a competing architecture.
2. Applicable Build Series
3. Core Implementation Invariants
Always build a household profile before generating any plan.
Always map user-facing cohort answers into backend personas and overlays; do not expose all backend personas in onboarding.
Always generate a 7-day class-first plan before selecting dishes.
Always keep primary family meal classes separate from member-specific add-on components.
Never allow kid, elderly, pregnancy, postpartum, diabetic, or recovery add-on classes to replace the main family meal unless the household itself has only that member segment.
Always apply hard constraints before scoring: diet type, allergies, Jain/fasting rules, and Never list.
Always use home state and current city as separate signals; do not collapse them into one location field.
Always use class-to-dish mapping keyed by meal_class_code; never mix dishes from unrelated meal classes.
Use v3 workbook IDs as canonical seed IDs unless a later approved source-data version supersedes them.
4. Overlay Principle

Health and lifecycle needs modify planning and add member-specific components. They should not automatically turn the whole household into a diet plan unless the household explicitly chooses that mode.
5. Overlay Types
6. Safety and Scope

The system should provide meal-planning personalization and not claim medical treatment. Health overlays must be described as preferences and food suitability filters. For serious medical conditions, the app can allow user-entered restrictions but should avoid diagnosis.
7. Ranking Effects
Diabetic overlay: demote sugar-heavy classes and boost low-GI add-ons.
Fitness overlay: boost protein-rich dish candidates and high-protein snacks.
Elderly overlay: demote very spicy, deep fried, and hard texture dishes.
Pregnancy/postpartum: use explicit safe/avoid flags as hard or strong constraints once defined.
Child overlay: boost familiar, mild, portable, and tiffin-friendly classes.
8. Implementation Output

health_overlays = [  {"type": "DIABETIC", "target_member_id": "elder_1", "strength": "hard_for_member_soft_for_household"},  {"type": "INFANT", "target_member_id": "baby_1", "strength": "addon_required"}]
9. AI Coding Agent Instructions
Read DOC-00 first to understand build sequence and document dependencies.
Use this document when implementing: BUILD-02, BUILD-05, BUILD-07.
Treat all IDs in source workbook v3 as canonical seed data identifiers.
When logic and data conflict, follow the rule hierarchy: hard constraints > add-on separation > weekly class matrix > state/city overlay > dish scoring > randomization.
Do not create new meal class codes, persona codes, or enum values in application code. Additions must go through DOC-27 Admin CMS and DOC-28 governance.
Write unit tests based on the acceptance criteria in this document and DOC-25.
10. Acceptance Criteria
Health/lifecycle overlays do not incorrectly replace primary meals.
Member-specific constraints are applied to add-ons and relevant dish ranking.
Fitness and health preferences remain configurable and learn from behavior.
11. Change Log
tables: Defines overlays for diabetic, weight loss, high protein, gym/fitness, elderly digestion, pregnancy, postpartum, child growth, recovery, and modern healthy lifestyle patterns.
Claude / AI coding agent, product owner, backend developer, data operations, QA
Generate meal classes first; expand to dishes second; attach member add-ons separately.
Build dynamic onboarding, household/member capture, diet and city inputs, and profile object.
Attach infant/toddler/child/elderly/pregnancy/postpartum/diabetic/fitness add-ons without replacing family primary meal.
Use swipes, locks, Not Today, Never, search, and accepted repeats to adjust preferences and class weights.
Demote sweets/refined-carb-heavy/fried classes; prefer low-GI sides.
Low-oil sabzi, sprouts, curd side, methi/lauki/chana dal.
Prefer light dinners, protein snacks, controlled indulgence.
Increase protein frequency and post-workout snack classes.
Avoid unsafe foods; add nourishing mild components.
Recovery foods as add-ons, not family-wide primary.
Laddoo/panjeeri/ajwain/gond type components where regionally suitable.
Soft khichdi, curd rice, dalia, steamed veg.
Initial canonical technical document generated from agreed v3 workbook and documentation architecture.
